description: Create a Copier-based FastAPI service template at enterprise-patterns/python/enterprise-pattern-fastapi/ and replace the schema-registry stub with a service generated from it.

userStories.title: Create Copier template configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a copier.yml configuration file so that new FastAPI services can be generated with parameterized settings. Create enterprise-patterns/python/enterprise-pattern-fastapi/copier.yml with _subdirectory: project, _min_copier_version: 9.1.0, _envops for clean Jinja output, _skip_if_exists for alembic/versions/*.py and CHANGELOG.md, _tasks for chmod +x entrypoint.sh, and questions with validators for service_name (kebab-case), module_name (Python identifier), service_description, service_port, service_version, use_database, use_gunicorn. Also create the project/{{ _copier_conf.answers_file }}.jinja answers file template recording _src_path, _commit, and all answer values.
acceptanceCriteria: copier.yml exists at enterprise-patterns/python/enterprise-pattern-fastapi/copier.yml
_subdirectory is set to 'project'
_min_copier_version is set to '9.1.0'
_envops has trim_blocks and lstrip_blocks set to true
_skip_if_exists includes alembic/versions/*.py and CHANGELOG.md
_tasks includes chmod +x entrypoint.sh with OS guard
service_name question has kebab-case regex validator
module_name question has Python identifier validator and derives default from service_name
use_database and use_gunicorn are boolean questions with true defaults
Answers file template exists at project/{{ _copier_conf.answers_file }}.jinja with all required fields

userStories.title: Create template project files — core modules
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the core template files so that generated services have a complete FastAPI project structure. Create the project/ subdirectory with Jinja2-templated files: main.py (app factory with lifespan, exception handlers, router includes), config.py (pydantic-settings with Environment enum), exceptions.py (AppException hierarchy with handlers), schemas.py (base model with icontract example), dependencies.py (get_settings with lru_cache, get_db_session conditional), routers/health.py (health and version endpoints), __init__.py, py.typed, Dockerfile (FROM tdp-python-base), .dockerignore, requirements.in, requirements.txt, README.md with post-generation checklist, entrypoint.sh with exec for signal propagation, and logging_config.py with dictConfig JSON formatter and OTel trace/span injection.
acceptanceCriteria: project/src/{{ module_name }}/main.py.jinja exists with FastAPI app factory, lifespan context manager, exception handler registration, and router includes
project/src/{{ module_name }}/config.py.jinja exists with pydantic-settings BaseSettings, Environment enum, and SettingsConfigDict
project/src/{{ module_name }}/exceptions.py.jinja exists with AppException base class, NotFound/BadRequest/PermissionDenied/NotAuthenticated subclasses, and registered handlers
project/src/{{ module_name }}/schemas.py.jinja exists with base Pydantic model and icontract @invariant example
project/src/{{ module_name }}/dependencies.py.jinja exists with get_settings() using @lru_cache and conditional get_db_session()
project/src/{{ module_name }}/routers/__init__.py and routers/health.py.jinja exist with /health and /version endpoints
project/Dockerfile.jinja uses FROM tdp-python-base and adds only service-specific layers
project/.dockerignore excludes tests, __pycache__, .copier-answers.yml, BUILD.bazel, README.md, *.pyc
project/requirements.in.jinja and requirements.txt.jinja exist with appropriate dependencies
project/entrypoint.sh.jinja uses exec for signal propagation
project/logging_config.py.jinja uses dictConfig with JSON formatter injecting OTel trace_id and span_id
userStories.notes: Large effort task (EFFORT: L). All template files must use Jinja2 syntax compatible with Copier's _envops settings.

userStories.title: Create template project files — OTel instrumentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want OpenTelemetry instrumentation included in generated services so that every service starts compliant with platform observability standards. Create src/{{ module_name }}/telemetry.py.jinja with TracerProvider, OTLPSpanExporter, Resource configuration, FastAPIInstrumentor, conditional SQLAlchemyInstrumentor, HTTPXClientInstrumentor, and exported tracer example. Add OTel dependencies to requirements.in.jinja.
acceptanceCriteria: project/src/{{ module_name }}/telemetry.py.jinja exists
TracerProvider configured with OTLPSpanExporter
Resource set with service.name, service.version, deployment.environment from settings
FastAPIInstrumentor auto-instrumentation configured
SQLAlchemyInstrumentor conditionally included when use_database is true
HTTPXClientInstrumentor configured for outgoing calls
Exported tracer = trace.get_tracer(__name__) for custom spans

OTel dependencies added to requirements.in.jinja: opentelemetry-api, opentelemetry-sdk, opentelemetry-exporter-otlp, opentelemetry-instrumentation-fastapi, opentelemetry-instrumentation-sqlalchemy (conditional), opentelemetry-instrumentation-httpx

userStories.title: Create template project files — database (conditional)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want conditional database support so that services that need a database get async SQLAlchemy and Alembic setup automatically. Create database.py.jinja with create_async_engine (pool config), async_sessionmaker, expire_on_commit=False. Create models/__init__.py, alembic/env.py.jinja with async migration pattern, alembic/script.py.mako (verbatim, no .jinja), alembic/versions/.gitkeep, alembic.ini.jinja. Add database dependencies to requirements.in.jinja. Ensure lifespan in main.py disposes engine on shutdown.
acceptanceCriteria: project/src/{{ module_name }}/{% if use_database %}database{% endif %}.py.jinja exists with create_async_engine, pool_size, max_overflow, pool_pre_ping=True, async_sessionmaker, expire_on_commit=False
project/src/{{ module_name }}/{% if use_database %}models{% endif %}/__init__.py exists
project/{% if use_database %}alembic{% endif %}/env.py.jinja exists with run_async pattern and NullPool
project/{% if use_database %}alembic{% endif %}/script.py.mako exists (no .jinja suffix)
project/{% if use_database %}alembic{% endif %}/versions/.gitkeep exists
project/{% if use_database %}alembic.ini{% endif %}.jinja exists
Database dependencies added to requirements.in.jinja: sqlalchemy[asyncio], asyncpg, alembic, psycopg2-binary
main.py lifespan context manager disposes database engine on shutdown when use_database is true
userStories.notes: alembic/script.py.mako must NOT have .jinja suffix due to Mako syntax conflicts with Jinja2

userStories.title: Create template project files — testing skeleton
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a testing skeleton so that generated services include examples of all mandated testing patterns. Create tests/conftest.py.jinja with AsyncClient fixture, test database fixture (conditional), settings_override fixture, asyncio_mode=auto. Create test_health.py.jinja, test_schemas.py.jinja (Polyfactory example), test_properties.py.jinja (Hypothesis + icontract example). Add test dependencies to requirements.in.jinja.
acceptanceCriteria: project/tests/__init__.py exists
project/tests/conftest.py.jinja exists with AsyncClient fixture using httpx.AsyncClient + ASGITransport, conditional test database fixture, settings_override fixture, and asyncio_mode=auto
project/tests/test_health.py.jinja exists with /health and /version endpoint smoke tests
project/tests/test_schemas.py.jinja exists with Polyfactory model factory example and serialization round-trip
project/tests/test_properties.py.jinja exists with Hypothesis @given example and icontract @invariant + ViolationError test
Test dependencies added to requirements.in.jinja: pytest, pytest-asyncio, httpx, hypothesis, polyfactory, icontract

userStories.title: Create Bazel BUILD template
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an initial BUILD.bazel file so that generated services work with Bazel from the start. Create BUILD.bazel.jinja with py_library for source files, py_binary for service entrypoint, py_test for test files. Document in README.md that BUILD files are maintained by Gazelle post-generation.
acceptanceCriteria: project/BUILD.bazel.jinja exists with py_library, py_binary, and py_test targets
py_library covers src/{{ module_name }} source files
py_binary target for the service entrypoint
py_test targets for each test file
README.md.jinja mentions Gazelle for BUILD file maintenance

userStories.title: Create scripts/new-service.sh wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a wrapper script so that service generation is consistent and safe. Create scripts/new-service.sh that verifies working tree is clean in enterprise-patterns/, resolves template path from repo root, passes --trust --vcs-ref HEAD automatically, accepts service name as first argument, and prints a post-generation checklist.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/new-service.sh exists and is executable
Script verifies enterprise-patterns/ working tree is clean before proceeding
Script resolves template path relative to repo root
Script passes --trust and --vcs-ref HEAD to copier copy
Script accepts service name as first positional argument with usage message on missing arg
Script prints post-generation checklist mentioning Helm chart and release-please registration

userStories.title: Generate schema-registry from template
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the schema-registry service regenerated from the template so that it follows platform standards. Remove the existing monorepo/services/schema-registry/ stub and generate a replacement using scripts/new-service.sh. Add schema-specific endpoints (/schemas, /schemas/{name}) as service-level customizations in routers/schemas.py.
acceptanceCriteria: Old schema-registry stub is removed
New schema-registry is generated from the Copier template
.copier-answers.yml exists in the generated service with correct values
routers/schemas.py exists with /schemas and /schemas/{name} endpoints
All template-generated files are present and syntactically valid Python

userStories.title: Verify build and deploy cycle
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify the generated schema-registry builds and deploys correctly. Verify: docker build succeeds with FROM tdp-python-base pattern, bazel build works, Helm chart values align, service deploys to local k3d and responds on /health and /version, copier update works.
acceptanceCriteria: Python files in generated schema-registry pass py_compile
Dockerfile syntax is valid
BUILD.bazel has valid Bazel syntax
copier update --trust --vcs-ref HEAD --pretend runs without errors on the generated service
userStories.notes: End of main dependency chain — natural verification checkpoint. Full k3d deploy verification requires manual testing.

userStories.title: Verify release-please compatibility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify release-please still tracks the schema-registry component correctly after the restructure. Verify release-please-config.json and .release-please-manifest.json still reference the correct path, CHANGELOG.md is preserved, and conventional commits map correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: release-please-config.json references monorepo/services/schema-registry path correctly
.release-please-manifest.json includes schema-registry entry
CHANGELOG.md is preserved (covered by _skip_if_exists in copier.yml)
userStories.notes: End of main chain verification. Release-please config may need manual inspection.
